description_long: This object is a rectangular stereo sound system with a robust, black exterior casing. The front features a large speaker grille covering most of the surface, above which is a horizontal control panel with multiple rotary knobs, input jacks, and a prominent red power button. The top of the unit has a recessed handle for portability, while the sides and back are plain and solid, providing structural support. The rear view reveals an open section exposing the internal speaker cone, typical of many amplifier designs. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for both indoor and outdoor urban environments, such as parks, garages, or street performances. The design is utilitarian, focusing on function with minimal decorative elements.
description: A black, box-shaped stereo sound system with a front speaker grille, control panel with knobs and jacks, and a top handle for carrying.
description_view_0: The left side of the stereo sound system, showing a plain, solid black surface with no visible features.
description_view_1: The front view displays the speaker grille, control panel with knobs, jacks, and a red power button, as well as the top handle.
description_view_2: The right side of the stereo sound system, similar to the left, is plain and featureless.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals an open section exposing the internal speaker cone, with the rest of the casing remaining solid.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: The speaker grille is a large, flat, rectangular mesh with a fine, uniform grid pattern and no visible decorative elements.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel is a horizontal strip above the speaker grille, featuring multiple rotary knobs, input jacks, and a red power button, all evenly spaced and clearly labeled.
